issue_body:
The `z.string().url()` validator (specifically `z.httpUrl()`) incorrectly allows URLs with a single forward slash or no slashes after the protocol. For example, `http:/www.apple.com` and `http:example.com` both pass validation when they should fail.

Reproduction:

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

const httpUrl = z.httpUrl();

// These should all fail validation but currently pass:
httpUrl.parse('http:example.com');      // missing // after protocol
httpUrl.parse('https:example.com');     // missing // after protocol
httpUrl.parse('https:/www.google.com'); // missing one /
httpUrl.parse('http:/example.com');     // missing one /
```

All of these are malformed URLs that should be rejected. The native `URL` constructor silently normalizes them (e.g. `new URL('http:/www.apple.com')` returns `http://www.apple.com`), so the validator accepts inputs that are clearly invalid.
